intro_script: Welcome to your daily dose of robotics and automation news, July 11th, 2026. Today we cover autonomous freight launches, faster robot programming tools, and expanded AI safety platforms. Eleven updates await!
outro_script: And that’s your digest for July 11th, 2026. From Dallas to Houston, the bots are rolling! Thanks for tuning in—keep automating your day!

items.title: Tech company AVI-SPL launches autonomous Dallas-Houston freight operations with Volvo Autonomous Solutions
items.summary: AVI-SPL has launched autonomous freight operations between Dallas and Houston in partnership with Volvo Autonomous Solutions. These commercial runs utilize the Volvo VNL Autonomous truck, powered by Aurora Driver technology. The service supports AVI-SPL's transportation needs for various audio-visual electronics. This includes both new products and end-of-life equipment destined for precious metal reclamation.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Tech company AVI-SPL launches autonomous Dallas-Houston freight operations with Volvo Autonomous Solutions." The report details the initiation of self-driving truck services connecting the markets of Dallas and Houston. These commercial transports employ the specialized Volvo VNL Autonomous truck, operating under the guidance of Aurora Driver technology. The service is established to meet the specific transportation requirements of AVI-SPL's portfolio, handling both incoming new audio-visual electronics and outgoing end-of-life equipment designated for precious metal reclamation processes. This successful deployment marks a significant operational milestone demonstrating the viability of long-haul autonomous logistics solutions within commercial sectors.

items.title: RoboDK unveils CAM software that cuts robotic machining deployment time ‘by up to 40 percent’
items.summary: RoboDK, an offline programming and simulation software provider for robots, introduced RoboDK CAM software. This new tool allows users to generate complete robotic machining programs directly from CAD files. The software is designed to simplify the deployment of robotic systems in machining applications. RoboDK claims that using this CAM software can reduce the time required for robotic machining deployment by as much as 40 percent.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "RoboDK unveils CAM software that cuts robotic machining deployment time ‘by up to 40 percent’." This report details the release of new software designed to accelerate the integration of robots into manufacturing processes involving machining. The new tool allows users to generate entire robotic machining programs directly from existing CAD files, streamlining a traditionally complex programming workflow. RoboDK asserts that implementing this specific CAM capability can reduce the overall deployment timeline for such robotic systems by as much as forty percent. This development signals a measurable advancement in automating physical industrial cell setup within the broader automation technology sector.

items.title: FORT Robotics extends physical AI safety platform with Nvidia Halos
items.summary: FORT Robotics, known as the "Trust Layer" for physical AI, is joining the Nvidia Halos for Robotics ecosystem. This collaboration aims to enhance safety measures for autonomous robots. FORT will showcase an agentic safety application at the Automate conference in Chicago. The demonstration will utilize the open-source Nvidia Halos Outside-In Safety Blueprint. This presentation will take place within the Humanoid Robotics Pavilion.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "FORT Robotics extends physical AI safety platform with Nvidia Halos." This announcement details a collaboration intended to bolster safety protocols for autonomous robotic systems. The integration involves FORT Robotics, developer of the physical AI safety layer known as the Trust Layer, joining the established Nvidia Halos for Robotics ecosystem. During this partnership, FORT intends to present an agentic safety application at the upcoming Automate conference in Chicago. This demonstration will specifically employ the open-source Nvidia Halos Outside-In Safety Blueprint within the Humanoid Robotics Pavilion exhibit space. The industry can expect increased standardization and demonstrable adherence to advanced safety standards for embodied artificial intelligence systems moving forward.

items.title: How Path Robotics uses AI to optimize robotic welding
items.summary: Path Robotics leverages Artificial Intelligence to enhance the process of robotic welding. CEO Andy Lonsberry detailed these AI applications in his explanation. Additionally, UC San Diego professor Michael Yip provided insights into the field of robot learning. This information was initially published on The Robot Report platform.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "How Path Robotics uses AI to optimize robotic welding." This report details how Path Robotics is utilizing Artificial Intelligence to significantly advance the efficiency of automated welding processes. CEO Andy Lonsberry outlined several specific applications of this artificial intelligence within the scope of their operations. Furthermore, Michael Yip, a professor at UC San Diego, contributed expert perspectives concerning the expanding field of machine learning in robotics. The integration demonstrates a tangible advancement in industrial automation capabilities across multiple sectors. Further development suggests a trend toward greater autonomy and precision in advanced manufacturing systems.

items.title: XTEND secures U.S. patent for autonomous navigation technology
items.summary: XTEND has successfully obtained a new United States patent for its autonomous navigation technology. This newly acquired patent significantly enhances the company's existing intellectual property portfolio. The protected technology supports advanced functionalities including autonomous navigation systems. Furthermore, it bolsters capabilities related to operator assistance features. Finally, the patent coverage extends to mission execution protocols.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "XTEND secures U.S. patent for autonomous navigation technology." This report details XTEND's successful acquisition of a new United States patent covering its advanced autonomous navigation systems. The newly secured intellectual property significantly enhances the company's existing portfolio by providing legal protection across multiple operational facets. Specifically, the patented technology supports core autonomous movement capabilities while also bolstering features designed for operator assistance and comprehensive mission execution protocols. This development marks another notable expansion of protected technological infrastructure within the sector. Industry observers anticipate this patent coverage will further influence competitive standards for robotics hardware and software integration moving forward.

items.title: Fieldwork Robotics secures SEED Innovations investment to scale berry harvesting robots
items.summary: Fieldwork Robotics secured an investment from SEED Innovations, an AIM-quoted investing company chaired by Jim Mellon. This funding constitutes part of a £2.5 million Seed+ fundraise. The company develops selective, adaptive, and modular robots designed for harvesting berries. The financing combines both private investment and grant money. Announcement details were shared in April 2026.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Fieldwork Robotics secures SEED Innovations investment to scale berry harvesting robots." The story reports that Fieldwork Robotics has successfully obtained an investment from SEED Innovations. This funding is part of a £2.5 million Seed+ fundraise and solidifies the company's plans for expansion in automated agriculture. The technology itself involves selective, adaptive, and modular robotic systems engineered specifically for the task of berry harvesting. The announced financing structure incorporates both private capital injection and grant money sources. Details regarding this financial milestone were officially shared in April 2026. This development highlights growing investment interest in precision robotics applications aimed at optimizing specialized agricultural labor.

items.title: Cognibotics selected for €6.5 million in EU accelerator funding
items.summary: Swedish deep-tech robotics company Cognibotics secured €6.5 million in blended finance. This funding comes from the European Innovation Council Accelerator under the EU's Horizon Europe programme. The total investment comprises a €2.5 million grant and €4 million in equity financing. These funds will support the next phase of development for the company. Specifically, the money will aid in the industrialization processes undertaken by Cognibotics.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Cognibotics selected for €6.5 million in EU accelerator funding." The report details that Swedish deep-tech robotics firm Cognibotics has successfully secured substantial blended financial backing. This funding originates from the European Innovation Council Accelerator, operating under the scope of the EU's Horizon Europe program. The total investment structure incorporates a two point five million euro grant alongside an additional four million euros in equity financing. These combined resources are designated to support the next phase of the company’s advancement, focusing specifically on accelerating industrialization processes. This injection of capital signals continued European commitment to scaling advanced domestic robotics technology development.

items.title: Multi-robot demo showcases new UK’s Plymouth subsea test range
items.summary: A new underwater test site in Plymouth is designed to advance UK marine autonomy and ocean sensing. The site recently hosted a major demonstration featuring a live, multi-robot surface and subsea platform. This effort is part of the Smart Sound Connect Subsurface (SSCS) project. Leadership for the initiative comes from the University of Plymouth, collaborating with Plymouth Marine Laboratory (PML). The successful showcase marks the first significant test at the new facility.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Multi-robot demo showcases new UK’s Plymouth subsea test range." This report details a major demonstration at a newly established underwater testing facility designed to advance marine autonomy capabilities in the United Kingdom. The site recently hosted an event featuring a live operational sequence involving multiple surface and subsea robotic platforms. This demonstration is integral to the Smart Sound Connect Subsurface project, with the University of Plymouth leading the effort alongside collaboration from Plymouth Marine Laboratory. The successful execution marks the first substantial test conducted at this advanced facility. This infrastructure development establishes a significant testing benchmark for future advancements in underwater robotics and sensing technology within the sector.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Avoid dynamic loading of libraries (on Windows)
items.summary: A user is attempting to build a standalone ros2-godot plugin on Windows, requiring all necessary libraries to be embedded in the plugin directory. The core issue encountered involves dynamically loaded libraries that are not found because their containing directory is missing from the system PATH environment variable. While static linking for RMW and rcl_logging implementations was successful using CMake variables, a problem persists with type support DLLs loaded by `rosidl_dynamic_typesupport`. Specifically, the user seeks a method to prevent this dynamic loading of typesupport DLLs (like those for FastRTPS).
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Avoid dynamic loading of libraries (on Windows)". The discussion centers on a technical challenge encountered while building a standalone ros2-godot plugin targeting the Windows operating system. Users reported difficulties because necessary dynamically loaded libraries were not located due to missing directory entries within the system PATH environment variable. Although static linking proved successful for RMW and rcl_logging implementations utilizing CMake variables, persistent issues arose specifically with type support DLLs loaded by rosidl_dynamic_typesupport, necessitating a method to prevent this dynamic loading mechanism. The resolution provides technical guidance on restructuring dependency management processes within ROS 2 development environments for Windows portability. This finding represents an advancement in embedding complex robotics software into non-standard operating system builds across the embedded technology sector.

items.title: Richtech Robotics launches 24/7 interactive livestream featuring AI robot ADAM
items.summary: Nevada's Richtech Robotics has debuted a continuous 24/7 interactive livestream featuring its AI humanoid robot, ADAM. This new platform allows global audiences to interact directly with the advanced robot in real-time. Users can engage with ADAM by chatting and participating in activities streamed live from the broadcast. The initiative showcases Richtech's commitment to demonstrating their AI service robotics capabilities to a worldwide audience.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Richtech Robotics launches 24/7 interactive livestream featuring AI robot ADAM." This report details the debut of a continuous online broadcast showcasing advanced human-like artificial intelligence technology. The platform was established to enable global audiences immediate interaction with the sophisticated humanoid robot, ADAM. Users are able to engage directly through chat functions and participate in live activities streamed from the Nevada-based facility. The initiative serves as a demonstration of Richtech Robotics' extensive capabilities within the field of AI service robotics for international viewership. This development signals an increased accessibility model for showcasing advanced robotic interaction technologies across global markets.

items.title: Video Friday: A World Cup for Robots
items.summary: Features highlight advancements like two full teams of humanoid soccer robots playing an 11-vs-11 match. Demonstrations include an underwater/air flapping robot prototype and breakthrough 25-DoF hands with advanced tactile sensing. New developments involve generalist AI models achieving high success rates on physical tasks, alongside companion robots capable of fluent conversation. Industry leaders are presented, including Boston Dynamics showing Atlas performing feats at major sporting events. Finally, speakers emphasize the necessity for proactive national strategies addressing training and ethical frameworks to support humanoid robotics in real-world applications.
items.audio_script: From IEEE Spectrum - Robotics, "Video Friday: A World Cup for Robots." The report details significant recent advancements across various domains of humanoid robotics and artificial intelligence implementation. Demonstrations featured two complete teams of humanoid soccer robots competing in an eleven-versus-eleven match format. Further technological strides included prototypes demonstrating underwater to air flapping capabilities and the unveiling of twenty-five degree-of-freedom hands equipped with advanced tactile sensing mechanisms. Furthermore, generalist AI models were shown achieving high success rates on complex physical tasks, complementing companion robots exhibiting fluent conversational abilities. Industry representatives highlighted achievements such as Boston Dynamics presenting Atlas performing at major sporting venues. Ultimately, speakers stressed the necessity for proactive national strategies concerning training protocols and ethical frameworks to facilitate real-world deployment of humanoid robotics technology.
